name: Banana Chocolate Marble Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Grease a square baking pan.
3. In a bowl, combine flour and baking soda. Mix well and set aside.
4. In a separate bowl, blend sugar and butter using an electric mixer until smooth.
5. Add sour cream, mashed bananas, egg, and vanilla extract to the sugar mixture. Beat until smooth.
6. Gradually add the flour mixture to the banana mixture, blending just until combined on low speed.
7. Gently fold in the melted chocolate chips and orange zest. Stir until a marbled pattern develops. Do not overmix.
8. Pour batter into prepared baking pan.
9. Bake for 40-45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Allow cake to cool in the pan for 15 minutes, then invert onto a rack and allow it to cool to room temperature.
Ganache:
1. Scald cream in a saucepan and remove from heat.
2. Add chocolate chips and orange zest to the hot cream. Stir until blended and cover the pan.
3. Allow the ganache to set for about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Once set, stir the ganache until it is smooth.
5. Chill the ganache in the refrigerator until firm.
6. Turn the cake right side up onto a serving plate and frost with the cooled ganache.
